rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in the component type scope for input assumptions and output guarantees
Use only visible component ports in type-level contracts; avoid subcomponent paths
Preserve existing AADL architecture by inserting annexes in valid scopes
Do not use AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions; rely on visible ports or constants
generic_rules: Maintain architectural integrity by inserting annexes in appropriate scopes
Use only visible identifiers in contracts to ensure portability
Avoid implementation-specific details in component type contracts
Preserve existing connections and subcomponents when inserting annexes
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `assume` for input constraints and `guarantee` for output promises
Declare constants with `const` and equations with `eq`
Wrap AGREE contracts in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent paths in component type contracts
Avoid direct references to AADL properties in AGREE expressions
Do not place implementation-specific bindings in component type annexes
Avoid mixing component type and implementation annexes in the same block
